title: Ten Ways to Save the 2021 Academy Awards Mailbag Special
format: Sean and Amanda work through the Critics' Choice, DGA, and BAFTA nominations, predict the ten Best Picture nominees days out from the real announcement, and name who they'd add if they ran the Academy. Then a mailbag on ratings, format fixes, and a five-song Oscars medley, before Anthony and Joe Russo join to talk about Cherry.

segments.heading: Precursor Season: Critics' Choice, DGA, and BAFTA
segments.note: Nomadland's sweep continues, and BAFTA's new jury system produces a curiously all-white Best Film lineup

films.title: Nomadland
films.note: Wins Best Picture, Director, adapted screenplay, and cinematography at the Critics' Choice Awards; the hosts call it the clear front-runner heading into Oscar noms.

films.title: Minari
films.note: DGA nominee for Lee Isaac Chung; the hosts call it a lock.

films.title: Promising Young Woman
films.note: DGA nominee for Emerald Fennell and a BAFTA Best Film nominee.

films.title: Mank
films.note: DGA nominee for David Fincher; recognized by nearly every artisan guild despite lukewarm person-to-person enthusiasm.

films.title: The Trial of the Chicago 7
films.note: DGA nominee for Aaron Sorkin and a BAFTA Best Film nominee.

films.title: The Father
films.note: BAFTA Best Film nominee.

films.title: The Mauritanian
films.note: BAFTA Best Film nominee.

films.title: Ma Rainey's Black Bottom
films.note: Absent from BAFTA's all-white Best Film list despite the diversity elsewhere on the ballot.

films.title: Da 5 Bloods
films.note: Also shut out of BAFTA Best Film; Denzel Washington was eligible as a producer and still went unrecognized by BAFTA.

films.title: One Night in Miami
films.note: The third Black-led Best Picture contender left off BAFTA's Best Film list.

segments.heading: Predicting the Best Picture Ten
segments.note: working from the PGA's ten nominees, four days before the real Oscar noms

films.title: Nomadland
films.note: "Number one with a bullet": an easy lock.

films.title: Minari
films.note: Lock.

films.title: Ma Rainey's Black Bottom
films.note: Lock.

films.title: Mank
films.note: Lock, though Sean flags actors as the one guild not fully embracing it.

films.title: The Trial of the Chicago 7
films.note: Lock.

films.title: Promising Young Woman
films.note: Predicted in; its low rental price doesn't drop until the Tuesday after Oscar noms, which the hosts think will drive a fresh wave of discourse.

films.title: Sound of Metal
films.note: Predicted in on the strength of Riz Ahmed's performance, despite neither host loving the film outright.

films.title: One Night in Miami
films.note: Predicted in.

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: Sean's hopeful pick for the ninth or tenth slot, comparing its late-season surge to Phantom Thread's.

films.title: Borat Subsequent Moviefilm
films.note: The other contender for that last slot; Amanda doubts both Borat and Judas get in together.

films.title: News of the World
films.note: Discussed as a possible dark horse the PGA didn't nominate.

films.title: Soul
films.note: The PGA snub the hosts find baffling for a well-liked, critically strong Pixar film.

segments.heading: Under-the-Radar Picks: Who They'd Like to See Nominated
segments.note: Best Director, lead and supporting acting, and screenplay wishlists

films.title: Da 5 Bloods
films.note: Delroy Lindo's Best Actor snub is the one both hosts expect to generate the most outrage Monday morning.

films.title: Another Round
films.note: Sean's Best Actor pick for Mads Mikkelsen, who he calls at least plausible given the film's other recognition.

films.title: The Father
films.note: Anthony Hopkins would win Best Actor outright in a year without Chadwick Boseman, per Sean.

films.title: Malcolm & Marie
films.note: Amanda's Best Actress curveball for Zendaya, though she's fine with the film missing given Zendaya's career ahead of her.

films.title: French Exit
films.note: Amanda's hunch for a surprise Michelle Pfeiffer Best Actress nomination, despite the film not working for her overall.

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: Dominique Fishback for Best Supporting Actress, and Sean's pick for original screenplay off its WGA nomination.

films.title: Borat Subsequent Moviefilm
films.note: Amanda's adapted screenplay pick, also boosted by a WGA nomination.

segments.heading: Mailbag: Ratings, Rewrites, and What-Ifs
segments.note: listener questions on the postponed 2020 movies that might've been Best Picture contenders, and a stat-padding Avatar re-release

films.title: Avatar
films.note: Prompted by news of its China re-release chasing Avengers: Endgame's box-office record; Sean dismisses it as "stat padding."

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: Named as a postponed 2020 release that could've been in the Best Picture conversation.

films.title: The French Dispatch
films.note: Same list.

films.title: In the Heights
films.note: Sean's pick for the postponed film most likely to have been "in the conversation in a big way."

films.title: Dune
films.note: Amanda raises it as an open question: event movie or something with awards weight.

segments.heading: Mailbag: Amanda's Five-Song Oscars Medley
segments.note: a listener asks for five musical numbers pulled from film history, performed by contemporary artists

films.title: Breakfast at Tiffany's
films.note: Source of "Moon River"; Amanda's dream performer is Frank Ocean.

films.title: Meet Me in St. Louis
films.note: Source of "Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas"; Amanda picks Sturgill Simpson to sing it.

films.title: Skyfall
films.note: Amanda wants Adele back for the title song, at full volume.

films.title: Wayne's World
films.note: The Lemonheads' cover of "Mrs. Robinson," which Amanda would stage with Simon & Garfunkel visibly annoyed nearby.

segments.heading: Anthony and Joe Russo on Cherry

films.title: Cherry
films.note: Adapted from Nico Walker's semi-autobiographical novel, negotiated over prison phone calls with the incarcerated author. Stars Tom Holland, cast during the Captain America: Civil War audition process. The Russos structure the film in visually distinct chapters (romance, military training, war, addiction, incarceration), aimed at a Gen Z audience they see as processing images differently than prior generations.

films.title: Captain America: The Winter Soldier
films.note: Cited in the intro as part of the Russos' résumé.

films.title: Avengers: Infinity War
films.note: Cited in the intro.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: Cited in the intro as the highest-grossing film of all time at the time.

films.title: Extraction
films.note: Brought up by contrast with Cherry, a movie the Russos produced but didn't direct themselves.

films.title: The White Tiger
films.note: Joe Russo's last-great-thing-he's-seen pick, praised for its international storytelling.

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood
